ISEN 399 Syllabus

Section 500 (39434)

Professional Development

Spring 2026 - College Station

Course Information

Meeting Times:  Meeting Type: INS

Meeting Days:

Start Time: N/A

End Time: N/A

Start Date: 01/12/2026

End Date: 05/05/2026

Credit Hours:  0

Instructor Details

Jose Vazquez

Email:  vazquez@tamu.edu

Office Location:  Engineering Technology Building (ETB)

Room 3030

Phone:  979-574-1714

Office Hours

Tuesday noon-1:30

Preferred Contact Method

email

Biography

TAMU class of '75. Academic degrees: BA Pol Sci; MA

English Lit & Writing Pedagogies; MS National Security

Strategy. Veteran who served 27 years in the Army.

Catalog Description

Professional Development. (0-0). Credit 0. Participation in an approved high-impact

learning practice; reflection on professional outcomes from engineering body of

knowledge; documentation and self-assessment of learning experience at mid-

curriculum point. Prerequisites: Grade of C or better in ISEN 210 and ISEN 230;

senior classification.

Course Prerequisites

Prerequisite/Corequisite(s):  Grade of C or better in ISEN 210 and ISEN 230; senior

classification.

Course Learning Outcomes

Upon completion of this course, the learner will be able to:

Learn from an extracurricular experience

Apply course content as necessary

Fulfill a graduation requirement

Special Course Designation

None

This “course” fulfills the requirement for each undergraduate student in

the college of engineering to document their participation in a  high-

impact learning   practice . This requirement was established because of

the value it adds to a student’s learning and the likelihood that a hands-

on learning experience provides lessons that are more imbedded and

Special Course Designation

None

This “course” fulfills the requirement for each undergraduate student in

the college of engineering to document their participation in a  high-

impact learning   practice . This requirement was established because of

the value it adds to a student’s learning and the likelihood that a hands-

on learning experience provides lessons that are more imbedded and

more effectively reinforce classroom instruction—directly and indirectly.

Textbook and/or Resource Materials

This material Is: Recommended

AP Stylebook

Subtitle:  57th Edition

ISBN:  978-0-917360-71-8.

Grading Policy

The single submission for this course will be graded on a pass/fail (S/U)

basis.

All submissions in this course are via  Canvas . There is no need to use

sources in the paper you will write for this course, but if you borrow

content and do not know how to document borrowed content, ask me.

All submissions will be washed through  Turnitin.  If you have no

experience with the  Turnitin  software or think you will not get caught, I

strongly advise you not to take the risk.  Turnitin  has been around for at

least 10 years and checks submissions against its repository of papers and

the Internet. If you submit content that  Turnitin  flags and my inquiry

reveals academic dishonesty, expect to have an honors case and an F* on

your record. While the * can get removed if you successfully complete

remediation training, the “F” will not.

Late Work Policy

Work submitted by a student as makeup work for an excused absence is not

considered late work and is exempt from the late work policy ( Student Rule 7 ).

Course Specific Late Work Policy

There are three submission opportunities. If you miss one,

submit to the next opportunity. If you miss the last submission

opportunity, contact me ASAP.

Submission Dates

Submissiondates are 2/16, 2/23, and 4/20.

AI Statement

AI is allowed for reviewing content you produce. It is not allowed

for producing the essay.

The ISEN Department Faculty upholds the  Aggie Honor Code

as an axiom of our academic excellence. We consider its

sincere observance essential for membership in our

department and Texas A&M. We extend to you the trust

conferred to those who faithfully adhere to our honor code.

Abuse of this trust is intolerable; thus we will report and assign

an extreme penalty* to those who do not stand with us in

preserving the integrity symbolized by the  Aggie Honor Code ,

“An Aggie does not lie, cheat, or steal or tolerate those who

do."

Any work submitted for this course’s requirements constitutes

each student's certification that the entirety of the content and all

approaches, techniques, and applications utilized resulted from

only the student. There will be absolutely no tolerance extended to

any student found in violation of this policy or the

Aggie Honor Code .

*  In this course the penalty for any violation of the Aggie

Honor Code, regardless of how minimal the violation may

be, is an F*.  Incidents of academic dishonesty will be handled in

accordance with university  rules and procedures specified at

http://aggiehonor.tamu.edu .

Course Specific Attendance Policy

This course does not meet, so attendance is not an issue.
